mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a robust labor market, which supports strong consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are consistently negative or flat when UNRATE is below its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above a critical threshold forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a hawkish stance, increasing interest rates (FEDFUNDS) and raising the discount rate for equities, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.5% month-over-month for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:inflationary pressure leads to rate hikes
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances where the CPIAUCSL trigger is met.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based strength in consumer demand and investment, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.3% and HOUST 3-month average growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:consumer and housing strength drives economy
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST show strong growth, SPX 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) following an economic contraction signals a recovery in manufacturing and supply chains, leading to increased corporate output and profitability, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO shows positive month-over-month growth for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: During identified post-recession recoveries, INDPRO growth does not correlate with positive SPX 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and impending economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending and lower corporate profits, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points from its 3-month low
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances following a significant UNRATE increase.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which provides a strong foundation for corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth correlates with market performance
failure_condition: Periods of sustained GDPC1 growth are not associated with positive SPX 60-day returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and strong retail sales (RSAFS) indicates an accommodative monetary policy supporting robust consumer demand, creating a highly favorable environment for equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:low rates and strong demand boost stocks
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is low and RSAFS is growing, SPX 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat, with a Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth coupled with declining industrial production (INDPRO) suggests a potential late-cycle scenario where labor costs rise while goods production slows, leading to margin compression and inflationary pressures, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.2% and INDPRO month-over-month decline > 0.1%
evidence_refs: fact:divergence can signal late cycle stress
failure_condition: In late-cycle expansion, strong PAYEMS and weak INDPRO are associated with positive SPX 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.
